content: The spelling variant 'reflexion' (the less common British English spelling of reflection documented in the high school vocabulary curriculum) can be framed as a natural 'self-reflection perturbation' for training LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where forcing an LLM to process the non-standard orthography of 'reflexion' when invoking self-reflection routines encourages it to break out of rigid, over-learned reflection patterns. This small orthographic shift acts as a low-cost trigger to push the LLM to generate more diverse critical self-evaluations instead of repeating generic reflective outputs.
